title: BOOM Numeric Molecular OOD Mini-Audit Memory

resource_records.name: BOOM 10k density pilot files
resource_records.source: BOOM 10k DFT density and heat-of-formation data with OOD split labels.
resource_records.access_notes: This was a measured pilot, not a full BOOM reproduction. Fresh runs should fetch BOOM through upstream repository instructions.

similarity_axes.name: max_morgan_tanimoto_to_train
similarity_axes.definition: For each test molecule, compute maximum Morgan fingerprint Tanimoto similarity to the BOOM training split and evaluate error as maximum train similarity decreases.
quality_gates: Use a chemically meaningful fingerprint when RDKit is available.
Report ID versus OOD performance separately.
Label this as post-hoc fixed-training OOD subset evidence unless new SPECTRA splits are generated and models retrained.

findings.claim: Within the BOOM 10k density OOD split, lower chemical overlap to the training set corresponded to higher RMSE for a RandomForest Morgan-fingerprint baseline.
evidence: OOD RMSE increased from 0.2317 at max Tanimoto <= 0.8 to 0.2534 at max Tanimoto <= 0.5.
limitations: Post-hoc OOD subset curve rather than generated SPECTRA train/test splits.

reuse_guidance: Use this entry as a molecule-domain smoke test for SPECTRA curves and report formatting.
For a stronger molecular result, generate overlap-controlled train/test splits and retrain models per split.
If RDKit is unavailable, classify Morgan/scaffold axes as not evaluable rather than silently substituting a weaker string proxy.
do_not_repeat: Do not claim this pilot is a full BOOM reproduction.
Do not treat fixed-training OOD binning as the final SPECTRA benchmark mode.
Do not use this as evidence that vanilla agents cannot discover molecular OOD failure.
